commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes the Codex app-server dynamic-tool bridge to read tool entries and fields defensively, snapshot valid name/description/schema values for Codex specs, and add fuzzplugin regressions for unreadable entries, fields, and empty names.

PR surface: Source +114, Tests +101. Total +215 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main directly iterates and reads dynamic tool entries/fields before quarantine, so a Proxy entry or throwing getter can escape during bridge creation. I did not run tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is one narrow mechanical repair: align the new OpenClaw tool-name quarantine helper with Codex's validated dynamic-tool identifier contract.

target.title: fix(codex): quarantine unreadable dynamic tools
target.body:
## Summary

- quarantine Codex dynamic tools whose array entries or core fields throw during app-server projection
- snapshot the readable tool name/description/schema before registering specs so later spec creation does not re-read bad getters
- keep the existing unsupported-schema quarantine path, with added fuzzplugin regressions for unreadable entries and fields

Stacked on https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88649 so the test fixtures stay neutral.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (40 tests)
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts`
- AWS Crabbox `run_c4a7e1dc6754` / `cbx_e32bd5a31948` (`amber-crayfish`, `c7a.8xlarge`): `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed` passed; lanes `coreTests`, `extensions`, `extensionTests`; lease stopped
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/test-neutral-tool-schema-fixtures-refresh-20260531`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings, overall 0.82
- `git diff --cached --check`
- added-line private-name scrub: no matches

Behavior addressed: a malformed plugin/runtime tool object can no longer crash Codex app-server dynamic tool registration before a turn can produce content.
Real environment tested: local Codex extension Vitest lane plus AWS Crabbox changed gate.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts --reporter=verbose`; `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed` on AWS Crabbox.
Evidence after fix: unreadable dynamic tool entries, parameters, descriptions, and empty names are quarantined while the healthy `message` tool remains registered.
Observed result after fix: 40 dynamic-tool tests passed; formatter, oxlint, diff check, remote changed gate, autoreview, and added-line scrub passed.
What was not tested: full live assistant turn with a real external broken plugin; coverage uses synthetic fuzzplugin fixtures.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
